objective: Close F-0173 statically by enforcing one shared runtime scoring-point result contract at both the direct AI scoring service boundary and the durable scoring-task completion boundary, so only an exact one-to-one result for every persisted scoring_point can become scored.
blockedFiles: any schema or migration source, real database/data access, Provider call/configuration/prompt change, permission or quota change, dependency, deployment, secret/env, external service, PR or force-push

preRedImpactScan.authoritativeChain: mock_exam immutable scoring_point snapshot -> durable ai_scoring_task inputSnapshot -> governed executor resultSnapshot -> ai-scoring-task-runtime completion gate -> transactional repository finalization; direct ai-scoring-service runner result -> student-flow runtime adapter
preRedImpactScan.currentVulnerableSink: ai-scoring-service accepts duplicate, missing and unknown scoringPointPublicId values while ai-scoring-task-runtime validates only scalar score/status before durable completion
preRedImpactScan.securityInvariant: the returned scoring-point ids are unique and exactly equal to the persisted expected set; every point is normalized once against its own max score; unknown, duplicate, missing, malformed or non-finite results fail closed before scored persistence
preRedImpactScan.legitimateBehavior: valid one-to-one results retain 0.5 rounding, per-point caps, whole-question cap, durable provenance and retry/failure lifecycle semantics

requiredSemantics.singleContract: one runtime validator/normalizer shared by direct scoring and durable task completion; no duplicate acceptance algorithm
requiredSemantics.setEquality: expected and actual scoringPointPublicId sets must be non-ambiguous, unique and exactly equal; order cannot hide mismatch
requiredSemantics.runtimeValidation: do not trust TypeScript or executor output shape; reject malformed arrays, ids, booleans, scores, reasons and non-finite values
requiredSemantics.scoring: preserve half-point rounding and per-point max; total is the sum of exactly one normalized result per expected point and remains capped by authoritative question max
requiredSemantics.expectedFacts: direct uses context scoringPoints/maxScore; durable uses persisted task inputSnapshot scoringPoints/maxScore and validates it before executor execution
requiredSemantics.determinism: actual order is untrusted; canonical output follows authoritative expected order with ordinal case-sensitive identity matching
requiredSemantics.durableScalar: executor scalar score, max and scored status must equal the canonical point result and cannot replace point validation
requiredSemantics.durablePersistence: persist only an explicit allowlist result snapshot plus unchanged provenance; never spread unvalidated executor output
requiredSemantics.failure: invalid Provider/executor structure is a stable failed attempt, never scored, never completed and never persisted as a success result
requiredSemantics.replay: existing successful immutable result behavior remains read-only; no second Provider call or mutation
requiredSemantics.scope: no material context, answer-input limits, usage accounting, schema, permission, quota, prompt or Provider execution changes
